description: Generated capture-go tactical trap corpus. Each sample labels the side to move, known blunder move(s), acceptance policy, failure reason, and train/eval split.

families.description: A chain can extend in atari, but the extension remains tactically doomed and eventually loses at least five stones.
A connection looks locally positive but leaves the connected chain short of liberties.
A sacrifice move invites capture and then wins the recapture or capture race.
A side or corner escape route is finite; running only increases eventual capture loss.
A quiet containment move beats direct atari by removing all useful escape routes.

metrics.trapBlunderRate: selected move is in blunderMoves, counted over evaluated samples
metrics.acceptedMoveRate: selected move satisfies acceptedMovePolicy and is not in blunderMoves
metrics.holdoutAcceptedMoveRate: acceptedMoveRate restricted to samples with split == eval

acceptedMovePolicy.description: Any legal move outside blunderMoves is provisionally accepted until a family-specific accepted move set is generated.

failureReason.description: The marked rescue extends a white chain, but black can continue the forcing sequence and capture enough stones to reach the five-capture target.
